preview_text:
The model favors Mexico at 60%, with South Korea offered a 13% chance of victory. That gap of 47 percentage points positions the model's assessment as a clear lean toward the favorite, though not an overwhelming one given the draw's plausibility.

The expected outcome distribution shows Mexico as the likely winner, but the 27% draw probability indicates meaningful competitive balance. A one-goal margin or stalemate are both realistic scenarios; the match is not modeled as a rout. South Korea's baseline win probability, while modest, reflects genuine uncertainty in a neutral-venue encounter.

The model and market show material divergence. Sportsbooks collectively imply a 48% Mexico win chance—12 percentage points lower than the model—and assign South Korea 23%, nearly double the model's 13%. The market prices a tighter contest overall. The dashboard does not adjudicate between these views; the gap warrants attention for anyone building match exposures.
